name: Orange-Glazed Pork Loin
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Green grapes, seeded and halved
recipeInstructions:
1. Score the fat on the pork roast at 1-inch intervals. Place the roast with the fat side up on a grill directly over a drip pan prepared from three thicknesses of heavy-duty aluminum foil. Insert a meat thermometer through the fat into the center of the meat. Cover the grill and adjust dampers to cook at low heat (approximately 350°F) until the meat thermometer reads 170°F (allow 20 to 30 minutes per pound). Use mesquite chips to add aroma to the meat.
2. For the sauce, heat butter in a 1-quart saucepan. Stir in brown sugar. Add orange concentrate and stir until smooth. Remove 1/4 cup of sauce and baste the roast during the last 20 minutes of cooking time.
3. To complete the sauce, stir water into cornstarch. Gradually add to the remaining orange juice mixture. Cook, stirring constantly until thickened. Cook for an additional 8 minutes. Add grapes. Serve hot with pork.
